text: The surgeon takes down the end ileostomy and begins mobilizing the small bowel. The small bowel mesentery is dissected from the retroperitoneum, starting in the right lower quadrant and proceeding towards the ligament of Treitz. The free edge of the small bowel mesentery and the terminal ileum are visible.

In the pelvis, the rectal stump is identified and the mesorectal plane is entered. The transanal team places a purse string suture to close the rectum, followed by a full thickness proctotomy distal to the suture. The initial incision is made anteriorly, and dissection proceeds circumferentially to enter the mesorectal plane from below. Simultaneously, the laparoscopic team advances the dissection circumferentially, ensuring even progress in all quadrants to maintain the correct plane throughout.

The two dissection planes converge, allowing one team to see or feel the other team's approach. The light source from the other team aids in recognizing the correct plane. Once the planes are connected, the dissection proceeds circumferentially. The specimen is then completely freed and extracted transanally.

A purse string suture is placed on the anal cuff through the rectal port to position the circular stapler. An ileal J pouch is constructed at the old ostomy site, with the anvil placed at the bottom of the pouch before returning it to the abdomen. The peritoneum over the ileal mesentery is incised to reduce tension on the anastomosis. The stapler is advanced through the port and connected with the anvil to construct the anastomosis. The transanal team checks the integrity of the anastomosis, and a diverting loop ileostomy is matured at the old ostomy site.
